strategic_profile.executive_summary: Purdue University is a premier public land-grant research university renowned globally for its elite STEM programs, particularly in Engineering, Computer Science, and Aviation. The university has maintained unprecedented tuition freezes since 2012, significantly boosting its value proposition and applicant demand. Admissions have become increasingly selective, utilizing a holistic review process that heavily prioritizes academic rigor and quantitative metrics over soft factors.
strategic_profile.market_position: Public Ivy / Elite STEM Public Flagship
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic but highly numbers-focused, emphasizing academic preparedness, STEM coursework rigor, and competitiveness within capacity-constrained majors.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Overall acceptance rate fell to 43.4% for Fall 2025, but masks extreme selectivity disparities. Out-of-state applicants face a ~39-43% admit rate, while highly impacted majors like Professional Flight (7.7%), Nursing (28.9%), and Computer Science (42.9%) operate closer to elite private college selectivity standards.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants must view Purdue not as a reliable safety school, but as a highly competitive target or reach, especially if applying from out-of-state for a STEM discipline.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Purdue has frozen base tuition for 14 consecutive years, keeping in-state tuition under $10,000 and out-of-state tuition under $29,000.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Provides exceptionally high ROI and lower student debt compared to peer institutions, though merit aid for out-of-state students remains highly competitive.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The First-Year Engineering (FYE) program requires all incoming engineering students to complete a rigorous foundational curriculum before transitioning to specific engineering disciplines.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Admitted engineering students must maintain strong GPAs (typically 3.2+) during their grueling freshman year to ensure placement in their preferred engineering specialty.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant, traditional Big Ten campus life featuring robust Greek life, extensive student organizations, and a strong athletic culture, balanced by a rigorous academic environment.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located in a classic college town (West Lafayette) with strong campus shuttle systems; approximately an hour from Indianapolis and two hours from Chicago.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R1 Doctoral University with Very High Research Activity, featuring world-class facilities and significant undergraduate research opportunities in engineering and agriculture.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-expected/required. Standardized test scores (SAT/ACT) are required for all applicants beginning with the Fall 2025 admissions cycle, with exceptions only for extreme hardship.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist outcomes are extremely volatile depending on yield. Class of 2029 waitlist admit rate was unusually high.
Record low waitlist admit rate due to over-enrollment.

gpa.notes: Purdue recalculates and reviews grades on an unweighted 4.00 scale.

testing.policy_note: For Fall 2025, Purdue fully reinstated its standardized testing requirement, making scores practically mandatory.

colleges.name: College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for top-tier STEM students with heavy AP Calculus and Chemistry backgrounds. Applicants must apply directly to First-Year Engineering.
colleges.housing_profile: Access to Engineering Honors and specific living-learning communities.

majors.name: First-Year Engineering (FYE)
majors.special_requirements: All incoming engineering students must pass FYE with at least a 2.0 Engineering Admissions Index (EAI) to transition into a specific major, though popular majors require much higher.

core_courses: ENGR 13100 Transforming Ideas To Innovation I
MA 16100 Plane Analytic Geometry And Calculus I

majors.notes: Acts as a foundational gateway. To transfer (CODO) into FYE from another college, students need minimum 2.7 cumulative GPA, but practically 3.2+ due to space constraints.

colleges.name: College of Science
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for students targeting Computer Science or Data Science, requiring near-perfect math scores and extensive programming extracurriculars.
colleges.housing_profile: Traditional dorms with science-oriented learning communities.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Admitted on a strict SPACE AVAILABLE BASIS only for internal transfers.

core_courses: CS 18000 Problem Solving And Object-Oriented Programming
electives: CS 37300 Data Mining And Machine Learning

majors.notes: Extremely difficult to CODO (transfer internally) into; requires B or better in CS 18000 and Calculus, and overall GPA prioritizing 3.8+ due to space.

colleges.name: Mitch Daniels School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Great for students blending quantitative skills with management and business analytics interests.

majors.name: Business Administration / Marketing
notable_professors: Dr. Chad Allred (Clinical Associate Professor of Marketing)
Dr. Jinsuh Lee (Clinical Associate Professor of Marketing)
Dr. John Burr (Clinical Associate Professor of Strategic Management)

colleges.name: College of Health and Human Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Competitive for Nursing; highly rigorous biology and chemistry background required.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: Space available basis for internal transfers.
majors.notes: Internal transfer (CODO) into nursing requires exceptional performance in notoriously difficult BIOL 203/204 anatomy sequence.

colleges.name: Purdue Polytechnic Institute
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Excellent hands-on technology programs, though Professional Flight is ultra-competitive.

majors.name: Professional Flight Technology
majors.notes: Arguably the most competitive major on campus. Acceptance rate is lower than Ivy League averages.

application_deadlines.notes: Priority deadline for competitive majors (CS, Engineering, Nursing, Flight), merit scholarships, and the John Martinson Honors College.
Regular decision. Decisions roll out between January 15 and March 31.

supplemental_requirements.details: Purdue-specific short answers including a 'Why Purdue?' prompt.
Two additional 500-word essay prompts regarding interdisciplinary study and how the applicant will shape their honors experience.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Purdue tracks Demonstrated Interest to a degree (marked 'Considered' on CDS). Yield management is heavily monitored. Legacy status is explicitly marked as 'Not Considered'.

major_selection_tactics: Apply Early Action (Nov 1) absolutely. It is the only way to be seriously considered for impacted majors (Engineering, CS, Flight, Nursing) and merit scholarships.
Do not try to 'backdoor' into Computer Science or Engineering by applying to an easier major. Internal transfer (CODO) is strictly space-available and highly uncertain.
If interested in Computer Science but lacking perfect stats, consider applying to Data Science or Computer Engineering Technology in the Polytechnic Institute as a primary or alternate major.
college_ranking_tactics: Highlight heavy STEM coursework (e.g., AP Calc BC, Calc-based Physics, Chemistry). Purdue evaluates the quantity and rigor of math/science heavily.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Purdue allows applicants to select a primary and alternate major. However, the alternate major should not be an impacted major. If you select Computer Science as primary, choose a non-impacted STEM or Liberal Arts major as the alternate, as closed programs will not admit from the alternate pool.

financials.tuition_model: Flat-rate frozen tuition. Tuition has remained frozen since 2012-2013.

scholarships.name: Trustees Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable per year. $10k for in-state, $16k for out-of-state.

scholarships.name: Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable per year. $4k for in-state, $10k for out-of-state.

scholarships.name: Stamps Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers full educational costs plus $10,000 in enrichment funds for study abroad, research, or unpaid internships.

scholarships.name: Beering Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Full ride for Honors College students.

ap_policy.general_rule: Scores of 4 or 5 typically award specific course credit. Scores of 3 usually yield undistributed subject credit (e.g., 1XXXX).
exceptions: AP Physics requires a 5 to earn specific course credit; 3 or 4 yields undistributed credit.
AP Econ requires a 3 on one and 4/5 on the other for specific macro/micro credit.
ap_policy.usage: Can be used to fulfill prerequisites and core curriculum, allowing students to skip intro courses (useful for entering Engineering/CS).

ib_policy.general_rule: Accepts Higher Level (HL) exams with appropriate scores.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Grades do not transfer (except from Purdue regional campuses), only credit. Must earn C- or better in a non-remedial course.

what_it_takes: Heavy course rigor in high school, especially taking AP Calculus BC and Calculus-based Physics.
Applying Early Action (Nov 1) is virtually mandatory for competitive majors.
A strong unweighted GPA (typically 3.8+) that proves readiness for a grade-deflated, rigorous STEM environment.
essay_tips: The 'Why Purdue' essay needs extreme specificity. Mention exact courses, labs, and unique undergraduate research opportunities.
For Honors College, emphasize interdisciplinary studies and clearly bridge your technical skills with liberal arts or societal impact.
Highlight Midwestern work ethic and 'Boilermaker' grit.
red_flags: Applying Regular Decision for Computer Science or Engineering.
Lack of Calculus or Chemistry on the high school transcript if applying to STEM.
Treating Purdue as an easy safety school without demonstrating genuine interest.
insights: Purdue is renowned for 'academic rigor' (and occasional grade deflation in STEM), but the co-op and internship network is unparalleled.
Yield management is becoming a massive factor. They slashed waitlist admits down to 9 in 2024 due to over-enrollment.
CODO (Change of Degree Objective) into CS or Engineering is theoretically possible but practically a gauntlet due to space limitations. Do not bank on it.
